title: Today's Markets Brief: Cathie Wood Sells $12 Million in Surging Stock
summary: A look at Cathie Wood's $12 million stock sale and a dividend ETF for uncertain markets.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm taking a look at today's market headlines. Let's dive into some interesting moves in the finance world. I was catching up on some news over the weekend, and a couple of stories caught my eye. They're not earth-shattering, but they're definitely worth a quick chat.

In other news, investors are looking for ways to navigate uncertain markets. One option that's been gaining attention is a dividend ETF that's designed to perform well regardless of what the market does next. With global unrest, tariffs, and rising interest rates, it's no wonder investors are seeking out more stable options. This ETF's focus on dividend-paying stocks could provide a relatively steady income stream in turbulent times. I'm not surprised that investors are looking for ways to play it safe – who isn't a little nervous about what's coming next?

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.

sources.title: Cathie Wood sells $12 million in surging stock
The Dividend ETF Built for Whatever the Market Does Next
